title: Agent Coordination Lane Route v1
description: The bound model + reasoning effort (the "route") for a lane, emitted additively on the lane's claim, heartbeat, or lane-manifest record. Route is a lane attribute rather than a standalone keyed record, so it carries no logical key of its own; it inherits the identity of the record it rides on. A route is either the compact "model/effort" string (for example gpt-5.6-sol/xhigh) or the equivalent structured object; the two forms are interchangeable and canonicalize to the same model and effort. Route is optional: a producer that has no route omits the property, and the dashboard degrades an absent route to a hidden/em-dash chip. This schema defines state only; emit paths and UI behavior are separate concerns.

route_string.description: Compact route "model/effort" with exactly one slash and no whitespace, for example gpt-5.6-sol/xhigh or claude-opus-4-8/high. Neither part may be empty or contain a slash or whitespace. Use the structured form when a model identifier itself contains a slash.

model.description: Model identifier, for example claude-opus-4-8 or gpt-5.6-sol. Non-empty and whitespace-free; a slash is allowed here because the object form is unambiguous.

effort.description: Reasoning-effort label, for example xhigh, high, medium, low, or none. Non-empty and whitespace-free; the provider-neutral vocabulary is intentionally open.

route_object.description: Structured route. Canonicalizes to the compact string as model + "/" + effort.

route.description: A lane route in either interchangeable form.

lane_route_projection.description: Embeddable additive fragment for a claim, heartbeat, or lane-manifest record. The record's existing properties remain valid beside this section. route is optional; when present it must be a valid route. There is exactly one way to signal "no route" — omit the property — so route is never null. An absent route degrades to a hidden/em-dash chip on the dashboard.
